lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. You made it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, tell me about this proposal of yours.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Camdozaal is a shadow of what it once was. We Imcando Dwarves were the finest artisans in the world, but now I am all that remains. Today, you'll only find echoes of our once great city.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: But that which was once great can still be great again! We Imcando Dwarves know that better than most. There is hope for the future of Camdozaal. But I need help.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What do you need?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We have a Sacred Forge. You will find none other like it in all the world. Some of the greatest weapons of legend started life here.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Forge could be the key to Camdozaal's future, but it's going out. If it does, I fear it will never be lit again. I can no longer gather the fuel it needs. But perhaps you can?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Fuel?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Barronite. You'll only find it in these ruins. If you can gather some for me, I can use it to keep the Forge lit. In return, I'll let you take what you want from our Great Vault.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I no longer have use for anything in the Vault. Besides, material possessions mean nothing compared to the fire of the Sacred Forge.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So I find barronite in the ruins and bring it to you? Doesn't sound too difficult. Where should I start?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Anywhere you like. The ruins are filled with barronite, so anything you do here should yield some. If you need more information on specific things you can do, just let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the other Imcando Dwarves?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah... It's a tough tale to tell. Even though it's been many years since the last of my kin died out, the memory is still fresh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't have to tell me if the memory hurts too much.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No, I will. The legacy of my kin must live on.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: As you can see around you, Camdozaal was once a great city. Alive with the hum of activity. We dug deep into the mountain and reaped the rewards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Our methods would often differ from other dwarves. Whereas they just used traditional smithing techniques, we would combine them with magic as well.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alas, that was ultimately our undoing. We learned of a horde of barbarians pillaging across the land, hunting down and killing anyone who dared use magic.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We underestimated them. We believed our weapons would be enough to keep us safe. We were wrong.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The barbarians brought ruin upon Camdozaal. Most of my kind died as the city collapsed around them. Only a few of us survived until the end. Now, only I remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The world has changed a lot, Ramarno. Magic is practiced freely. Those barbarians failed to wipe it from Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm glad they didn't win, I suppose. But it doesn't change much for me. I am still the last Imcando Dwarf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sorry Ramarno. I will do my best to help ensure the legacy of your kin lives on.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]. That gives me some hope. If you have any more questions, do not hesitate to ask.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you later.

sections.title: If the player has met Thurgo as part of Knight's Sword

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I do here?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: As you can see, Camdozaal isn't the city it once was. That said, there are still a few things around here that might interest you. There's the golem workshop, the barronite mine and the offering altar.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the golem workshop.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You'll find it to the east. It was once the forefront of our technological advances. Sadly, it was also one of the major factors that led to our doom.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What were you doing there?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Camdozaal was built upon the ruins of an older civilization. Little trace of them remained by the time we arrived. However, when building the city, ancient golems were discovered.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: These golems were very crude. They were mostly made from clay, of all things. We used our advanced Smithing knowledge to improve their form. Eventually, we were able to get them working again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We kept pushing with the golems. Many of them we imbued with magical energies. We started with mind and body energy, but eventually moved on to chaos as well.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alas, the influence of chaos had grave results. We lost control of many. Those that became corrupt had to be locked away, but by then it was already too late.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The barbarians had learnt of our experimentation, and were determined to destroy all traces of it.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ironically, locking them away meant the golems were among the few things to survive the barbarians. If you fancy a bit of combat, you might want to go take a look.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: They're largely made of barronite now, so they're a good source of the stuff.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Their cores are also very interesting. Apparently, the dwarves of the golem workshop once used old cores to craft runes. Maybe you could do the same.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the barronite mine.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You'll find it to the north west. Unsurprisingly, it's the best place to find barronite in the city.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: At one time, our miners would work all through the day there to gather the barronite needed to power the Sacred Forge. That was a long time ago.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: If you're lucky, you might find other things while mining there. We were not the first to build a home beneath this mountain. Sometimes traces of an older civilization can be found.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kind of traces?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You might find some ancient relics encased within barronite. If you do, you can use the anvils here to crack open the deposits.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The relics are of no interest to me. Maybe some of your race would find them interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me more about the offering altar.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You'll find it to the south west. It's not something that ever interested me, but most of my kind were followers of Saradomin - the god of order and wisdom.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Many of my kin would visit the altar to make offerings in his name.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: First, they'd catch fish from the nearby reservoir. Next, they'd ritually prepare them at tables by the altar. Finally, they'd use the altar to offer the fish.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There's plenty of barronite to be found within the reservoir, so it might be worth checking out. Be warned though: worshipping gods never did my kind any good.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Let's talk about something else.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Very well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something I'd like you to examine.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well hand it over and I'll take a look.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What happened to the other Imcando Dwarves?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah... It's a tough tale to tell. Even though it's been many years since the last of my kin died out, the memory is still fresh.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You don't have to tell me if the memory hurts too much.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No, I will. The legacy of my kin must live on.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: As you can see around you, Camdozaal was once a great city. Alive with the hum of activity. We dug deep into the mountain and reaped the rewards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Our methods would often differ from other dwarves. Whereas they just used traditional smithing techniques, we would combine them with magic as well.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alas, that was ultimately our undoing. We learned of a horde of barbarians pillaging across the land, hunting down and killing anyone who dared use magic.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: We underestimated them. We believed our weapons would be enough to keep us safe. We were wrong.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The barbarians brought ruin upon Camdozaal. Most of my kind died as the city collapsed around them. Only a few of us survived until the end. Now, only I remain.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: The world has changed a lot, Ramarno. Magic is practiced freely. Those barbarians failed to wipe it from Gielinor.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm glad they didn't win, I suppose. But it doesn't change much for me. I am still the last Imcando Dwarf.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well... that isn't completely true.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: What do you mean?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: While adventuring, I met another Imcando Dwarf called Thurgo.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: An Imcando Dwarf survived outside the city?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. He looks to be doing quite well for himself. In fact, he seems to be a master of Smithing.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I can't believe it. I hope to meet him one day. He must be a truly amazing smith to reach such heights.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well he's certainly an interesting dwarf.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Thank you, [player name]! You've given me hope that my kin can live on. If you need help with anything else, let me know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can I use the Forge?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm afraid not. The Sacred Forge is like none other in existence. Only an Imcando Dwarf could use it correctly.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What makes it so special?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Truth be told, I don't fully know. It's far older than I am. It was supposedly built in the earliest days of the city.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: What I do know is that it's been imbued with powerful magic - some said that of the gods themselves. The Forge powers the entire city, or what's left of it at least.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I've been doing my best to keep it lit over the years. It's not a job I'm capable of any more though. That's why I'm hoping you can help.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Bring me any barronite you find. In return, I can channel enough power from the Forge into the city or even open the Great Vault.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will powering the city do?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: From what I can tell, my ancestors linked the Forge to the entire city.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: It truly is the heart of this place. From it, I can channel any barronite you have into empowering different aspects of the city.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What will that do?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Sacred Forge is a strange beast, but with its arcane power, you'll see an improvement to the various activities you can do in the city.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Would you like to take a look?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes please.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Very well. Anything else you'd like to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What is the Great Vault?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: It's one of the many marvels of this city. We used it to store our greatest creations, along with our most interesting finds.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Of course, I have no use for those treasures myself. I'd happily give them up to keep the Sacred Forge alive.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Now be warned - I won't be able to channel much power into the Vault. I can't afford to waste too much barronite. You'll only have a limited amount of time in there.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Not only that, there's an automatic security system that activates if power to the Vault is lost. If that happens, you'll be removed. I imagine any treasures would be lost as well.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How much barronite do you need to power it?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Hmm, for a minute, I suppose 750 barronite shards should do the trick.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: If you do go in, I'd suggest heading to the back first. That's where the most valuable things are kept.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Is there anything else you wanted to talk about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll see you later.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Never mind, I have no questions for now.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'll be here if you change your mind. Take care [player name].

sections.title: If the player has started Knight's Sword but not yet met Thurgo

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Can you make me a knight's sword?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: A sword? Why do you need me to make you one?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I was told to find a Imacando dwarf to make a sword for me.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm afraid there's not much I can do to help you [player name]. My lack of vision means I can't work an anvil like I used to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh... that's a shame.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: They were probably talking about someone else anyway. I don't think many surface dwellers remember the dwarves who lived in Camdozaal.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Right, I'll see if I can find someone else to make one for me then.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this encased within some barronite.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, that looks like one of the relics from the civilization that lived here before Camdozaal was built.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Do you want it?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I have no use for it. Maybe one of your kind would find it interesting.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hmm. Maybe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found one of these deposits while mining.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, a barronite deposit. You can use the anvils here to crack it open. If you're lucky, it might have something inside.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have some barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I could feed those shards into the Sacred Forge now, and keep their power for you to use later if you'd like?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What can I use the power from the Forge for?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well there's a few things we could do with that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well just bring it back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this bit of barronite.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: That looks like part of a weapon. Hmm, I might be able to make something out of it. You'll need to have all the right pieces... and 1,500 barronite shards or if you'd prefer, I can smelt it down for Barronite shards?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You need a Barronite [piece] and 1500 barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Come back with all the parts, and I'll happily knock something up for you.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You have all the parts I need. Give me a second, and I'll fix it up for you.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go, one Barronite Mace. Should come in handy when killing the golems around here.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 100 Barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go. I can add those shards straight to the Forge as well if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, that'd be great.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go, that's all done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto them.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No problem, come back to me if you find anything else of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you, I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll hold onto it for now.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well just bring it back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What's this mace good for?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I reckon you could give one of those golems a nasty whack with that thing.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: If you have no use for it, I could smelt it down for some Barronite shards?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 500 Barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go. I can add those shards straight to the Forge as well if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, that'd be great.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go, that's all done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto them.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No problem, come back to me if you find anything else of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you, I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I found this broken hammer within a barronite deposit.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, the Imcando Hammer. A very useful tool for Smithing and Construction. I could repair it for 1,250 barronite shards if you like or if you'd prefer, I can smelt it down for Barronite shards?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You need 1250 barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Come back with the necessary items, and I'll happily repair the Imcando Hammer for you.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Give me a second, and I'll fix it up for you.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: All done! Here is your Imcando Hammer.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 350 Barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go. I can add those shards straight to the Forge as well if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, that'd be great.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go, that's all done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto them.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No problem, come back to me if you find anything else of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you, I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Well just bring it back if you change your mind.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: The Imcando Hammer, one of my favourite tools for Smithing and Construction.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: If you have no use for it, I could smelt it down for some Barronite shards?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Alright then, I can smelt that down for 500 Barronite shards.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go. I can add those shards straight to the Forge as well if you'd like.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Go ahead, that'd be great.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: There you go, that's all done.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto them.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: No problem, come back to me if you find anything else of interest.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thank you, I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No thanks I'll hold onto it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I've brought you a redberry pie.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Imcando dwarves love redberry pies. I thought you'd like one too.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Can't say I've ever had one.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Oh, well why don't you try it?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: So, what did you think?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: This is delicious! Where did you find such a excellent cuisine!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I made it myself.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: You're an brilliant cook [player name]! Just don't bring me too many of these, I'm trying to watch my weight.

sections.title: After Desert Treasure II - The Fallen Empire

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello again.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ah, [player name]. I'm sure you have plenty of questions. I will do my best to answer them.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I have something to tell you. It's about another dwarf.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Another dwarf? An Imcando Dwarf?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Yes. I encountered someone I believed to be a dwarf, but it was a lie. It was a Mahjarrat in disguise as someone called Ketla.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: Ketla...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: You knew her?

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I did, long ago. Is she...

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: She's gone, Ramarno. I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I see...

lines.speaker: Ramarno
lines.text: I'm sorry, [player name], but let us not speak of this further. To learn of the death of another of my kin is no surprise, but it pains me all the same. I would prefer to focus my thoughts elsewhere.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Of course.
